_patch_meta.description: C17 Dimension Review — Part 1: Reg 23 (compassion, 22 groups) + Reg 34 (covenant, 32 groups) + Phase B.5 rewrites (4 groups)

set.context_description: Reverential awe before the holy — the ark as the mediating sign that orients the inner person toward the presence and faithfulness of God
set.notes: Revised during Dimension Review Phase B.5: characteristic-perspective rewrite. Old: The ark as the visible sign of God's covenant presence and faithfulness

set.context_description: Reverential awe before the holy — the ark as the mediating sign that orients the inner person toward the presence and faithfulness of God

set.context_description: Obedience under judgment — the ark as the instrument that separates the inner orientation of faithful response from heedless disregard
set.notes: Revised during Dimension Review Phase B.5: characteristic-perspective rewrite. Old: The ark as means of preservation amid judgment — framing inner-being contrast of obedience and heedlessness

set.context_description: Obedience under judgment — the ark as the instrument that separates the inner orientation of faithful response from heedless disregard

set.context_description: Divine covenantal faithfulness — God's unbroken inner commitment expressed through the promise of dynastic continuity

set.context_description: Inner commitment under solemn self-curse — the cutting ritual as the enactment of the will's binding to covenant terms, with transgression named as the inner failure it judges

set.context_description: Inner commitment under solemn self-curse — the cutting ritual as the enactment of the will's binding to covenant terms, with transgression named as the inner failure it judges

operations.description: Group 767-001: sync context_description after Phase B.5 rewrite

set.notes: Compassion here is a stable inner character quality of the human person — the disposition of tender regard toward vulnerable others. The verse reveals the quality by its negation under catastrophe. Automated label Relational Disposition is correct. Toward-another orientation confirmed.

Chesed as divine steadfast love is the paradigmatic Relational Disposition — God's settled, enduring inner orientation of covenantal love toward his people. Dominant subject is GOD — these verses are about divine character. The 16-verse corpus confirms the pattern. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

This group captures the human quality of chesed — the settled inner character of covenantal loyalty. It is both Moral Character (a quality of stable character) and Relational Disposition (oriented toward another). The distinction: this group emphasises chesed as something God requires and desires as a character quality of the person, not primarily as an emotive orientation in a specific relational

This group is not primarily about chesed as a quality but about the human posture of appeal and meditation toward God's chesed. The inner characteristic is the trusting, dependent orientation of the human person who rests their hope in God's steadfast love. Automated label Relational Disposition is close but misses the directionality — this is not chesed as an outward disposition toward others, bu

The verse and description both describe a mirroring principle: the inner character of the human (merciful, blameless) draws out the corresponding inner character of God toward them. Neither pole dominates — the dynamic is genuinely correspondent. The automated label Moral Character named only one pole; Divine-Human Correspondence more precisely captures what the verse shows: a structural relations

Divine compassion as the inner movement of mercy toward the needy — this is God's relational orientation of mercy actively expressed. The group has only 2 verses with 1 anchor, so the corpus is small. The description is grounded. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but the verse does not show the human-divine mirroring structure — it is purely God's compassion toward Lot. Correc

God's freely given grace as his inner disposition of favour toward the undeserving. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but these verses are about divine disposition without a corresponding human mirror structure. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD. Note: this term (H2603B cha.nan) is also present in Reg 23 as the term for the human act of seeking grace (2325-002); the tw

The human act of seeking grace is fundamentally the posture of creaturely dependence — the inner orientation of humble plea directed upward. Automated label Relational Disposition captures the toward-another quality but misses the direction and asymmetry. The characteristic is the human creature's posture of reliance and appeal toward God from a condition of need — this is Dependence/Creaturelines

The settled disposition of graciousness — a stable inner character orientation of favour freely extended. The description correctly notes it applies "primarily [to] God." The 13-verse corpus and anchor verses confirm this. Relational Disposition correctly names the toward-another quality; Divine-Human Correspondence is not appropriate here since no human mirroring is described. Automated label Rel

The inner orientation of mercy and generosity directed toward those in need — this is a toward-another relational disposition expressed in action. Luk 11:41 grounds this directly in "what is within." Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

The pitiable inner condition — wretchedness, spiritual destitution, blind self-satisfaction. This is closest to Emotion — Negative as the inner state of a person in a condition of deprivation and unawareness. The UNCLASSIFIED automated label is being corrected to Emotion — Negative. However this sits at the boundary with Vitality/Existence (the person's inner constitution is deficient) — Emotion —

Both anchors show pity as an inner disposition — Psa 72:13 (human king) and Jon 4:11 (God). The group encompasses both human and divine pity. With 8 verses the corpus likely spans both. Dominant-subject: NONE — the group is genuinely dual-subject. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

The withholding of pity is itself an inner disposition — the deliberate suppression of the compassionate impulse in the service of justice. Both human (Deu 19) and divine (Eze 7) subjects are anchored. Dominant-subject: NONE — dual. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed; the negative form of the disposition (its suppression) is still a relational orientation.

Sympathy as the settled character quality of being responsive to others' suffering — this is a Relational Disposition, not Agency/Power. The automated label ROOT_INFERRED was a weak association that missed the character-virtue context. Correcting from Agency/Power to Relational Disposition.

Shared suffering as an expression of relational union — the inner disposition to genuinely participate in another's pain. This is Relational Disposition: toward-another orientation expressed as inner solidarity. Automated Agency/Power label corrected.

Controlled, empathetic gentleness arising from shared vulnerability — the capacity is in service of a relational orientation toward the weak and wayward. While the term has a "capacity" dimension (metriopatheō = to have measured/moderate feeling), the description and verse ground it in relational empathy, not in the exercise of power or authority. Relational Disposition is the better fit. Correcti
Deep, intensely felt divine compassion as a character attribute — God's inner quality of being profoundly moved toward those who suffer. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

The womb metaphor grounds divine compassion in bodily intimacy — God as the one who carries from the womb. The existing automated label Divine-Human Correspondence requires assessment: is there a human counterpart here? The verse is pure divine speech; the human-divine parallel is implicit in the womb image (human mothers carry children from the womb; God carries Israel the same way). The correspo

The visceral movement of compassion in response to suffering — with 12 verses this corpus spans both divine (Jesus as God incarnate) and human subjects. The anchor verses both show Jesus, but splanchnizō in parables (e.g. the father in Luke 15, the Good Samaritan) shows human subjects too. Dominant-subject NONE — genuinely dual across the full corpus. Automated label Relational Disposition confirm

Divine compassion as sovereign inner disposition — God's free inclination toward the objects of his mercy. This is pure divine relational orientation; no human mirroring structure is present. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is not appropriate; the verse is about the sovereignty of divine compassion, not a correspondence between human and divine. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GO

The compassionate inner movement of comfort — divine tenderness arising from within, described viscerally in Hos 11:8. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed. Note: Hos 11:8 is analytically exceptional — it names the divine inner life with a specificity rare in Scripture. The session B pointer below captures this.

The inner capacity for fellow-feeling — the disposition to share another's suffering. Heb 4:15 grounds this in Christ's genuine solidarity with human weakness across the divine-human boundary. This group has only 2 verses. Dominant-subject NONE — Christ is the anchor subject, who is both divine and human. Automated label Agency/Power corrected to Relational Disposition.

The inner orientation evoked by the ark's disclosure in a theophanic context is reverential awe before the holy — the creature's posture before overwhelming divine presence and faithfulness. This is Dependence/Creatureliness (the inner posture of the creature before God's holy majesty), not Moral Character (the automated label) and not Divine-Human Correspondence (though the divine presence is wha

The inner orientation of obedience versus heedlessness — the decision made during the time of warning. Volition captures the inner act of willing/choosing/refusing that the verse foregrounds. The automated label Volition is correct and the B.5 description aligns with it.

Faithlessness as a stable inner character quality — the disposition that cannot hold to covenant commitments. Moral Character is correct: this is a character disposition, not a relational orientation in the specific-encounter sense. The UNCLASSIFIED label is being corrected to Moral Character.

Divine covenantal faithfulness as the stable inner commitment of God toward his covenant partner — this is God's sustained relational orientation of loyalty expressed through promise. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but Jer 33:17 does not show a mirroring or correspondence structure; it shows unilateral divine commitment. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.

The act of covenant-making as the expression of God's relational commitment — the formal initiation of the bond. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed. GOD is dominant — it is God who initiates and makes the covenant.

The inner act of willing total commitment before God — "with all his heart and all his soul." This is the exercise of the will in binding commitment. Volition is more precise than Relational Disposition here: the description foregrounds the inner act of choosing/committing rather than the orientation-toward-another. The UNCLASSIFIED label is being corrected to Volition.

The inner act of binding the will to false objects — the disordered commitment that chooses deception and death as refuge. This is Volition (the act of choosing/binding) directed toward disordered ends. The UNCLASSIFIED label is corrected to Volition.

Intimate personal loyalty formalised in covenant — the inner-being orientation of love and commitment between persons expressed as a covenant bond. Relational Disposition is correct: the covenant here is the formalisation of an inner relational orientation. The UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Relational Disposition.

The formal severance of the relational bond — the legal expression of a failed inner relational orientation. Relational Disposition confirmed (specifically its severance/rupture form). Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

The inner-being severity of apostasy expressed through the divorce metaphor — Israel's transgressions as the inner failure that ruptured the covenant. The dominant subject is the human inner failure (apostasy) that caused the rupture. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but the verse focuses on the human side of the rupture; correcting to Relational Disposition, HUMAN.

The divine oath as the expression of God's unbreakable inner commitment — the binding of the divine will to covenant promise. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but Deu 7:8 does not describe a mirroring structure; it describes God's inner commitment enacted in redemption. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD. Note: 30 verses is a large corpus; the anchor is well-chosen

The binding of inner will and loyalty through oath — the moral quality of keeping commitments under cost. Psa 15:4 describes this as a character quality of the person who may dwell with God. This is Moral Character (stable inner quality of integrity) more precisely than Divine-Human Correspondence (which would require a mirroring structure). The ROOT_INFERRED label is corrected to Moral Character.

The oath as the expression of inner allegiance — its moral quality determined by whether it arises from truth, justice, and righteousness. This is Moral Character: the inner quality of the person determines the character of their oath. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is not appropriate (no mirroring structure); correcting to Moral Character.

Intense longing and love expressed through the adjuration — the inner state of love-sickness is the controlling characteristic. The automated label Volition (choosing, binding) misses the emotional register entirely. The inner state described ("sick with love") is Emotion — Positive (intense, consuming delight-longing). Correcting from Volition to Emotion — Positive.

The divinely appointed programme for the inner-being completion of the person and community — sin ended, iniquity atoned, everlasting righteousness arrived. This is Transformation (the movement from sin/iniquity toward righteousness as the inner-being trajectory). The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is not appropriate (the weeks are a programme, not a mirroring structure). Correcting t

The weeks as the temporal measure of sustained inner mourning and self-denial. The inner characteristic is the sustained grief/mourning state. Emotion — Negative is the appropriate dimension. The ROOT_INFERRED label (Divine-Human Correspondence) is clearly misassigned; correcting to Emotion — Negative.

God's unconditional inner commitment sustained by divine memory and will — a settled relational orientation of faithfulness toward creation and the patriarchs. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but Gen 9:15 shows no mirroring; it shows unilateral divine commitment. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.

The bilateral covenant structure requires inner decisions on both sides — God's faithful commitment and the human's obedient response. The inner characteristic is the volitional commitment on both sides: love, obedience, commandment-keeping as acts of will. Volition is more precise than the automated label Relational Disposition, which misses the enacted-commitment dimension. Dominant-subject NONE

Inner transformation through the inscribing of God's law on the heart — the paradigm of inner renewal in the Hebrew Bible. Automated label Transformation (KEYWORD_STRONG) confirmed. The transformation is enacted on the human person (DOMINANT-SUBJECT: HUMAN), though it is entirely God's act ("I will write").

God's everlasting covenant of steadfast love — a sustained divine relational orientation expressed as permanent commitment. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed. GOD is dominant — it is God who makes and sustains the covenant.
Covenant as the instrument of inner moral integrity — binding the self to a standard of purity. The anchor verse is Job's moral self-covenant. Automated label Moral Character confirmed.

God's covenant-bond with humanity established through Christ's blood — a divine relational commitment expressed at ultimate cost. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed. GOD is dominant — the covenant is God's initiative, established through Christ.
Inner transformation through the writing of God's law on the mind and heart — the NT fulfilment of Jeremiah's new covenant promise. Automated label Transformation (KEYWORD_STRONG) confirmed.

The inner-being condition of covenantal exclusion — hopelessness, alienation, separation from God. The description captures an inner state (the condition of being outside the covenant), and the verse names its inner-being texture: hopelessness and godlessness are the experiential markers of covenantal alienation. Emotion — Negative is more precise than the automated label Transformation (ROOT_INFE

The solemn binding of the will through the self-curse of the cutting ritual — the enacted commitment. The UNCLASSIFIED label is corrected to Volition: the characteristic is the inner act of the will binding itself under solemn terms, with the moral failure of transgression as its counterpart.
Moral consequence — the cutting off of wickedness as the judicial outcome of inner moral failure. Automated label Moral Character confirmed.

The solemn binding of the inner will through oath — the communal act of covenantal commitment. The UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Volition: the inner act is fundamentally the will binding itself to covenant terms.

The covenant curse as the consequence of inner moral failure — transgression and disobedience activating the covenant's judicial dimension. Moral Character confirmed: the inner moral failures named are character dispositions (willful disobedience, turning aside). Automated label Moral Character confirmed.
The firm covenant as the inner act of solemn communal commitment — the will directed toward binding covenant obligation in response to grace received. UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Volition.
Inner transformation through the Spirit as the defining character of the new covenant. Automated label Transformation (KEYWORD_STRONG) confirmed.
Inner-being renewal through the new self in Christ — the putting on of the person recreated in God's likeness. Automated label Transformation confirmed.

The eschatological identity of the person — the hidden new name that constitutes the person's ultimate inner-being identity before God. This is Vitality/Existence: the constitution of the inner person at the eschatological level, the person's truest and most intimate self-before-God. The automated label Transformation (ROOT_INFERRED) is close but misses the identity/constitution dimension — the ne

_patch_summary.note: No registry stamps in Part 1 — stamps applied in Part 4
